message:
feat(event_stream): tick 191 EventWaiter::wait_with_cx recv loop uses recv_cx (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Inside EventWaiter::wait_with_cx the recv_loop was using legacy
subscriber.recv() (ambient cx). The outer timeout_with_cx bounds the
overall wait by cx, so cancel still lands — but only via drop-cancel
of the inner future when the outer timeout wrapper sees cancel.

Tick 191 threads cx into the inner recv itself by routing through
subscriber.recv_cx(cx) (events.rs L736, which uses
broadcast_recv_with_cx). Each individual recv now observes cx
explicitly at its own await boundary rather than relying on outer
wrapper drop-cancel. Under LabRuntime virtual time this makes the
cancel land at the recv boundary deterministically rather than at
whatever point the outer timeout_with_cx polls next.

Both branches of the WaitCondition match (AllOf and default) are
migrated. Outer `timeout_with_cx` preserved as the timeout bound.

Legacy recv_loop using plain recv() was removed — no legacy-path
sibling exists here because wait_with_cx is itself the cx-first
entry point; the legacy `wait` variant still lives elsewhere using
its own recv loop and is untouched.

Verification: 48 / 48 event_stream:: tests pass under
--features asupersync-runtime via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang,
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target).

Note: events::tests::stats_clear_oldest_lag_when_queue_is_drained
is a pre-existing flake on main and is not caused by this change
(verified by stashing + rerunning on bare main).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
